name: 19 April: The Default Citizen
description: The Default CitizenCaste, Minority, and the Idea of Citizenship in IndiaJoin us for a lecture that begins with a deceptively simple question:Who is seen as a natural citizen, and who must constantly prove they belong?About the LectureWe often think of citizenship as a legal category, defined by documents, rights, and recognition by the state.But citizenship is also a matter of imagination.Who is assumed to belong without question? And who is treated as a minority, required to negotiate, justify, and defend their place within the nation?This lecture explores the idea of citizenship in India through its entanglement with caste, majority, and minority. Drawing on B. R. Ambedkar’s reflections on the minority question, the session shifts the conversation beyond religion and numbers, asking what happens when caste is placed at the centre of how we understand belonging.The talk examines:Why minority in India cannot be reduced to numerical smallnessHow historical subordination and unequal representation shape political belongingThe emergence of a “default citizen” whose presence is seen as natural and unquestionedHow caste and majoritarian politics shape the very idea of citizenshipRather than treating citizenship as neutral or universal, this lecture reveals it as a deeply political construct, shaped by power, history, and the ongoing struggle for recognition.About the SpeakerVikas Kumar Moola is a doctoral researcher in the Humanities and Social Sciences at Indian Institute of Science Education and Research Mohali, where his work examines the conceptual and historical making of citizenship in India through the lens of caste and majority-minority relations.He holds a Master’s degree in Sociology from the University of Hyderabad and an M.Phil. in Social Sciences from Centre for Studies in Social Sciences Calcutta.Alongside his academic work, he is part of the editorial team at The Ambedkarian Chronicle, contributing to the development of an autonomous anti-caste knowledge institution. He has also worked in the development sector, combining socio-political analysis with grounded field research.Vikas is a Central Committee member of the All India Independent Scheduled Castes Association (AIISCA), where he is engaged in advancing Dalit rights, dignity, and collective assertion across regions and communities.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue — so you can listen, reflect, and stay for conversation.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: Basement, Ground floor and First floor Building no. 12, Panchsheel Community Centre, Shahpur Jat, Panchsheel Park, New Delhi, Delhi 110017, India

name: 12th April: Who Gets To Laugh in the City?
description: Who Gets to Laugh in the City?The Politics of Dalit Women’s LaughterJoin us for a lecture with a simple but unsettling question:who gets to laugh in a city, and whose laughter is policed?About the LectureLaughter is often seen as spontaneous, harmless, even universal.But in deeply unequal societies, even laughter is structured.Drawing on ethnographic fieldwork conducted between 2022 and 2024 in Delhi’s urban villages, this lecture explores how Dalit women’s laughter operates as a powerful, everyday form of resistance. Moving beyond dominant narratives that centre grief and victimhood, the session foregrounds laughter as an embodied practice, one that navigates and subverts the intersecting violences of caste, gender, class, and religion.The talk examines how expressions that are often dismissed as loud, excessive, or vulgar carry political weight. Dalit women who laugh openly are frequently labelled “Daat Faadu Mahilaye”, women who “show their teeth", a phrase that reveals how caste and patriarchy work together to regulate not just bodies, but affect itself.About the SpeakerShainal Verma is a Ph.D. scholar in the Department of Humanities and Social Sciences at Indian Institute of Technology Delhi. Her research focuses on Dalit women’s oral histories, spatial negotiations, and everyday lived experiences in urban villages.Her broader interests include gender, affect, and anti-caste urbanism. Her work on Dalit women’s laughter received an Honourable Mention at the Bluestone Rising Scholar Competition (2026), and she has been a fellow at programmes including the Institute of Critical Social Inquiry (The New School), Writing Urban India, and Blue Club Media.Her writing has appeared in platforms such as Economic and Political Weekly, Himal Southasian, Scroll, and Feminism in India. Alongside her academic work, she has been actively involved in institutional student bodies, including ICC and SC/ST grievance committees.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue—so you can listen, reflect, and stay for conversation.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who want learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: Basement, Ground floor and First floor Building no. 12, Panchsheel Community Centre, Shahpur Jat, Panchsheel Park, New Delhi, Delhi 110017, India

name: Dalit History Month: 5th April Lectures
description: Join us for an evening of two back-to-back lectures that examine caste not only as a system of social organisation, but as a lived structure of power, embedded in institutions, relationships, and the mind itself: Social Justice is Void Without Addressing Caste & Gender (with Beena Pallical, 4:00 – 5:00 PM)The Cognitive Structures of Caste-Based Contempt (with Rahul Sonpimple, 5:30 – 6:30 PM)Social Justice is Void Without Addressing Caste & GenderAbout the LectureSocial justice is often spoken about as an abstract ideal, a principle to aspire toward. But without addressing caste, it remains incomplete.This lecture examines how caste and gender are not separate systems of inequality, but deeply intertwined structures that shape access, opportunity, and control. From regulating sexuality to determining who has access to resources and dignity, these systems operate together to sustain social hierarchies.The session explores:Why any meaningful idea of social justice must centre casteHow caste and gender mutually reinforce each other in everyday lifeWhy annihilation of caste requires not only policy change, but personal and societal introspectionAbout the SpeakerBeena Pallical is General Secretary at the National Campaign on Dalit Human Rights, and a leading voice in India’s social justice movement.Her work focuses on advancing inclusive and intersectional public policy, with particular attention to caste and gender-based discrimination. Through sustained advocacy and engagement, she has been instrumental in shaping conversations around rights, representation, and structural inequality in India.The Cognitive Structures of Caste-Based ContemptAbout the LectureCaste is often understood as a social or institutional system. But what if it is also a psychological one?This lecture examines how contempt, subtle, normalized, and often invisible, operates as a core mechanism sustaining caste hierarchies. It argues that practices such as the invisibilisation of Dalits in public spaces are not incidental, but foundational to how caste consciousness is produced and maintained.Drawing on sociology and psychology, the session explores:How caste-based contempt is socially cultivated and internalisedWhy exclusion is reproduced through everyday habits and perceptionsHow these patterns become part of a collective social psycheBy unpacking these processes, the lecture invites us to confront how deeply caste is embedded in how we see, think, and relate to one another.About the SpeakerRahul Sonpimple is a writer, social advocate, and political thinker working on caste, identity, and social justice in India. He is the founding President of the All India Independent Scheduled Castes Association (AIISCA) and Director of the Savitribai Phule Resource Centre.He holds a Master’s degree from the Tata Institute of Social Sciences and an MPhil and PhD in Sociology from Jawaharlal Nehru University. His work bridges academic research with lived realities, contributing to both scholarly and public discourse on caste.Dr. Sonpimple has written for platforms including HuffPost, Al Jazeera, and The Hindu, with academic work published in EPW and SAGE Journals. He is also the founder of The Ambedkarian Chronicle, a platform dedicated to critical engagement with caste and social justice.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue — so you can stay, reflect, and continue the conversation.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who want learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Shringaar Rasa & Ashta Nayikas
description: Note: The Venue is on 4th floor terrace and has no elevator. So stairs have to be climbed.जागू मैं सारी रैना बलमा, रसिया मन लागे ना मोरा ।बहुत रमायो सौतन घरवा, पिया गरवा लगावो ॥Meaning: Beloved, I stay awake the entire nightO playful one, my heart finds no peaceYou have spent much time at the other woman’s houseCome now, embrace me, my beloved.A night of longing, love, and lyrical emotion.Set in Raag Maru Bihag, this bandish evokes the Viraha in love through Virahotkanthita - a Nayika, a lady, a heroine, restless in separation, yearning for her beloved whom she suspects has lingered with the other woman far too long!!Through a curated selection of Khayal-Bandish(es), the artist brings alive the timeless world of the Ashtanayika - the eight heroines of love and separation across Indian Arts and aesthetics. Each composition unveils a different shade of desire, anticipation, and, longingness, anger.... weaving together music, poetry, and expression into an intimate, immersive experience under a moonlit night sky, overlooking the iconic Lotus Temple!Note: By attending you agree to being filmed at the venue and content may be used for marketing purposes of Team TBT-DBF

name: Partition & Delhi heritage walk @Purana Qila
description: Join Us for Partition & Delhi heritage walk & Baithak at Purana Qila on 19th April,2026From nuclear proliferation to terrorism, the Partition of India continues to cast its shadow even today. Join us for a Baithak that seeks to understand the complex experiences of Partition—an event that remains deeply misunderstood both within and beyond the subcontinent. Through facts, poetry, literature, photographs, paintings, and personal belongings, we will revisit this significant chapter of history. Discover how Partition shaped Delhi’s culture and internal geography.Kuch Pal Purana Qila Ke Naam! (suitable for 10 years & above)*Date: 19th April, 2026 Time: 8.30 am* Duration: 2 hours approx * Language: A blend of Hindi, Urdu & English(Special invitation to senior citizens, as this session involves minimal walking!)* Baithak Host: Nitika-Meeting point: Purana Qila ticket counter --Nearest metro station: Supreme Court- Instagram: Darwesh_taleweavers

Her art and life were deeply intertwined—each canvas echoing her inner world, her questions, and her fierce individuality.Join us for an intimate open-air baithak at Qudsia Bagh, where we explore her life through her artworks and conversations around the woman behind the canvas.(Earlier held at the National Gallery of Modern Art, this much-loved baithak now finds a new setting under open skies)🗓 12th April, 2026⏰ 8:30 am to 10.30 am📍 Qudsia Bagh, near Kashmiri Gate- Nearest metro station : Civil Lines & Kashmere GateAge suitability : Appropriate for 12 years & above.Instagram : darwesh_taleweavers

name: Martian Tales From a Former NASA Engineer
description: We’re bringing you Nimisha Mittal, a veteran space systems engineer with over 15 years of experience at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL). She led operations and testing for some of the world’s most ambitious robotic missions including the Cassini mission at Saturn, three of NASA's five Mars rovers, the Insight Mars lander, and most recently the NASA-ISRO SAR mission, NISAR. After a career of exploring the solar system, she's just completed her own mission of relocating to India. With engineering degrees from UT Austin and Stanford, she's now turning her attention from exploring other worlds to helping build the future of the Indian space industry.Her talk, Martian Tales from a Former NASA Engineer, dives into the thrill of driving robots across another planet and the bigger question of why Mars captivates us so deeply. Is it the search for life, the dream of a second home, or something even more profound? With insider stories from her time on the Mars mission, she takes us through what a day on the Red Planet really looks like, the challenges of remote space exploration, and even what it takes to earn a Martian Driver’s License.Come find out, and bring your questions — from whether humans can truly settle on Mars to what snacks power a NASA engineer through a 12-hour shift.All of this, over beer or mocktail at The Piano Man, Eldeco Centre! 🍻What to Expect:A 60-minute lecture on the realities of life on the Red Planet, from exploration to insider NASA stories15–20 minutes of interactive Q&AOne beer or a mocktail included with your ticketA lively mixer to share your insights📌 Note: Alcohol will be served only to guests aged 25 and above. Non-alcoholic options will be available. Attendance implies consent to being filmed for event documentation.Event Details:📅 Saturday, 18th April | 4 PM – 6 PM📍 The Piano Man, Eldeco Centre, Block A, Shivalik Colony, Malviya Nagar, New Delhi, 110017🎫 Limited to 150 attendees only💰 Early bird pricing available for the first few ticketsAbout the LecturerNimisha Mittal is a veteran space systems engineer with over 15 years of experience at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), where she led operations and testing for some of the world’s most ambitious robotic missions including the Cassini mission at Saturn, three of NASA's five Mars rovers, the Insight Mars lander, and most recently the NASA-ISRO SAR mission NISAR. After a career of exploring the solar system, she's just completed her own mission of relocating to India. With engineering degrees from UT Austin and Stanford, she's now turning her attention from exploring other worlds to helping build the future of the Indian space industry.About Pint of ViewInspired by the successful Lectures on Tap community in the U.S., Pint of View brings this format to India for the first time. It’s where big ideas meet bar tables, and where you enjoy a drink in hand while walking out with a new way of seeing the world.Book now & develop your own pint of view! 🍻

name: Night at the Museum - Humayun’s Museum Walk
description: A heritage walk through the country’s first sunken museum at the Humayun’s Tomb complex, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, brings together the story of Humayun with lesser-known layers of his life, while tracing the heritage of Nizamuddin over the past seven centuries.At its centre sits the finial of the Mughal monument, dislodged during a storm in 2014. The museum itself draws from the form of medieval baolis, with Humayun’s remarkable life unfolding through paintings, scrolls and artefacts.It also reconstructs the evolution of the 14th-century Nizamuddin Dargah complex through films and immersive technology, placing the site within a wider historical timeline.Located at the entrance to the World Heritage precinct, the museum acts as a bridge between Nizamuddin, Sunder Nursery and Humayun’s Tomb.Architectural models, original design plans of the tomb’s first floor, rare paintings, and carefully crafted replicas of chhatris and dome ornamentation sit alongside live demonstrations of building crafts. Stonework, tile work and incised plaster are presented alongside other Mughal traditions such as metalwork, textiles and manuscript painting.The museum also reflects on the changing landscape around Humayun’s Tomb, with the project realised by the Aga Khan Trust for Culture on behalf of the Archaeological Survey of India as part of a larger urban renewal effort.

location.address: H6VX+8G Humayun's Tomb, Nizamuddin, Nizamuddin East, New Delhi, Delhi 110013, India

name: #lecture24: You Die a Little Everyday
description: #lecture24 When was the last time you sat in a bar and thought about the fact that parts of you are dying—right now?Every day, billions of your cells quietly die and are replaced. This slow, invisible churn is not a flaw, but the very process that shapes how you age. What feels like continuity is, in fact, constant change.In this lecture, we turn inward.“You Die a Little Every Day” explores what aging really means—not as something distant, but as something already unfolding within you. Through simple, intuitive analogies, the session will unpack how your metabolism acts as the body’s engine, how different kinds of cell death shape your tissues, and how small imbalances today can quietly build into larger shifts over time. Moving between biology and lived experience, the talk invites you to see your body not as fixed, but as something continuously negotiating with itself.Touching on themes of aging, repair, and the fine line between balance and breakdown, this lecture asks: what does it really mean to grow older—and can we change how that story unfolds?We’re delighted to present Dr. Parul Mehrotra, Assistant Professor and Lab Head at the Immunometabolism Lab at IIT Delhi. Her work focuses on understanding how cell death, metabolism, and immune responses interact—particularly in the context of aging and disease.Having previously worked at institutions such as the Max Planck Institute for Biology of Aging and VIB Belgium, her research brings together cutting-edge science with questions that sit at the core of human life: how we change, adapt, and endure over time.What to Expect:✦ 4:00 PM – Doors open. Arrive early to grab your seat; seating is first come, first served.✦ 4:00 – 4:15 PM – Settle in, order your drinks and food, and ease into the space with a few conversations.✦ 4:15 PM – The lecture begins.✦ 5:00 PM – We open the floor for questions and a shared discussion with the speaker.✦ 5:30 PM onwards – Stay back for more food, drinks, and conversations that linger.📌 Note: Alcohol will be served only to guests aged 25 and above. Non-alcoholic options will be available. Attendance implies consent to being filmed for event documentation.

name: The Ministry of Resting Poetics
description: A poetry writing workshop on the theme of rest, revival & forgiveness.What does rest look like in poetry? How have poets and artists held space for stillness and rigor—together—in their work over the years? How does poetry as a process of writing, reading, and reciting subvert the false ideas of rush, urgency, productivity, and lead us to something remotely glinting as self-compassion?A two-hour-long poetry writing workshop comes right at the end of your mad week - a week fraught with deadlines, unanswered emails, and impromptu meetings — not only as a reminder of rest & forgiveness but a gentle reflection on them.Dillipoetry, with over a hundred poetry walks/workshops in the past years, has been melding poetry into themes that stir, pucker, and reprieve—bringing together a community of individuals who see poetry as a raging possibility amidst the resigned world.

name: Sip and paint tulips - by The Turtle art club

We are so excited to welcome you into our first event inside the Turtle Art Club - and initiative to inculcate slow joyful living in our everyday life. 🐢This Saturday, sit down for a relaxing evening and unwind with the energy of budding tulips in this season of spring and love , making something with your hands - for self or loved ones.Bring your friend or sister along as we learn how to paint sweet greeting cards, love notes, and bookmarks.Supplies will be provided. Come as you are! <3Who is it for:Anyone who wants to paint slowly, softly, and without pressure.Anyone who wants to have a good time by themselves or with their loved oneAnyone who wants to release stress from the weekAnyone who loves to re-connect with their inner child through artAnyone who is interested in creating meaningful handmade giftsPS: Enjoy shop the art collection by artists at 20% off!

name: The Quiet Grandeur: Tales of Trees and Tombs

Beyond the manicured lawns of Sunder Nursery lies a 16th-century saga waiting to be told. Join us for a heritage walk through the Azim Bagh, where we swap the city noise for tales of Mughal architecture, restored grandeur, and the secrets of the trees & monuments that stood the test of time.The walk is part of the March Tree Festival organised by SeasonWatch.

name: Overthinkers Anonymous
description: OverviewEver wondered why you default to people-pleasing, perfectionism, or avoidance? This session is about connecting the dots between your past experiences and your present reactions in a safe, judgment-free environment.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community-led initiative dedicated to creating safe, mindful spaces. Through workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities, from mental well-being to personal growth, with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectPattern Recognition: Explore why tendencies like overthinking or control keep showing up in your life.Guided Reflection: Use a specialized worksheet to gain context on your reactions without the weight of self-judgment.Awareness & Compassion: A gentle space to build self-awareness and understanding—focused on insight, not on reliving trauma.Details🗓️ Date: 12th April (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Thamel, Golf Course Ext Road🎟️ Contribution: ₹700 (Includes Coffee + Snacks)

name: The Procrastireaders

name: South American Supper Club On a Farm
description: Welcome to V’s Supper Stories, Supper Club — where food is slow, stories are shared, and the table is the experience.Every edition of V’s Supper Stories is rooted in a different mood — a story told through ingredients, plating, and pacing. The current theme?Tucked away from the chaos of the city, this Latin American supper club at The Farm Collective, Gurgaon invites you to slow down and savour an evening that feels both grounding and indulgent.Set against a serene farm backdrop, this sundowner experience (5:00 PM – 7:30 PM) is designed as a gentle escape from the noise and rush of everyday life. As the sun dips, you’ll be welcomed into a thoughtfully curated dining experience that brings together bold Latin American flavours with the simplicity of fresh, seasonal produce.Expect dishes crafted with farm-to-table ingredients, where vegetables are harvested fresh and transformed into vibrant plates that celebrate both flavour and origin. The menu reflects a balance of rustic authenticity and creative expression — rooted in Latin American traditions, yet shaped by the rhythm of the land around you.More than just a meal, this is an invitation to pause, connect, and experience food in its most honest form — shared over a table that values community, conversation, and the joy of eating well.Come for the sunset, stay for the feeling.Follow along at @vsupperstories for glimpses of the table, the food, and the people who bring it to life.

name: Before You Blame
description: Before You Blame: A Workshop on Navigating Relationships with ClarityOverviewMost relationship conflicts are not just about what happened. They are about how we interpret what happened. "Before You Blame" is a reflective workshop designed to help you look at your assumptions and inner patterns to bring more steadiness to your connections with others.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectPattern Recognition: Explore how common emotional triggers and reactions shape your conflicts in marriage, family, or work.Internal Focus: Instead of trying to fix others, we gently shift the focus inward to understand our own responses and ego patterns.Practical Tools: Learn simple and actionable ways to question stressful thoughts and practice perspective shifts using real life scenarios.Safe Exploration: A supportive and non judgmental environment for honest discussion and emotional awareness. This is a space for learning and not a lecture or therapy.Details🗓️ Date: 5th April (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Kanchanjunga Towers, Sector 56🎟️ Fee: ₹900 (Introductory offer; Original price ₹1100)

name: CUTTACK SPECIAL EVENING AT GRUHA
description: Step into an evening of nostalgia and flavour as Gruha brings alive the vibrant street food culture of Cuttack. From the iconic comfort of dahi bara aloo dum to a thoughtfully curated chaat experience inspired by bustling old-city lanes, the supper table celebrates the soul of Odisha’s food traditions. The highlight of the evening features the beloved pairing of soft chakuli and slow-cooked mutton curry, a meal deeply rooted in home kitchens, festive gatherings and Sunday indulgence.This supper experience is designed as a warm coming together of community, memory and regional taste — where every dish tells a story of streets, celebrations and shared heritage.

Expect a multi-course menu that moves through the flavours of Bengal—mustard, posto, seasonal vegetables, and slow-cooked meats—presented with a modern twist that reflects the spirit of Toontooni’s Table. Seats are intentionally limited to keep the experience personal and conversational. Whether you come solo or with a friend, expect an evening of slow dining, warm hospitality, and the joy of discovering Bengali cuisine in a setting that feels like home.Come hungry, curious, and ready to meet your table.

name: ALT EFF Film Club - Gurugram | 17th April
description: If you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 10 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation.With snacks. Always snacks.APRIL CURATION: "The Climate Baby Dilemma"Synopsis: "The Climate Baby Dilemma" by filmmaker Victoria Lean follows science writer Britt Wray as she explores the decision to have children amid climate change. Over three years, she engages with activists and parents, reflecting generational concerns. Featuring Sarain Fox and Severn Suzuki, the film offers insights on transforming climate anxiety into action.PROGRAMME17th April 2026, Friday7:00 - 9:00 PM: The Climate Baby Dilemma | Victoria Lean | Canada | 44 mins | English | 2022All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.What's Included?Film screeningGroup discussionLight refreshmentsCommunity spaceStudent Discount50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening). CODE: ALTSTUDENTABOUT ALT EFFAll Living Things Environmental Film Festival (ALT EFF) is the world’s largest decentralised film festival and a premiere showcase for environmental cinema.Across its annual festival, monthly Film Club, schools initiatives, film fund, and year round curations, ALT EFF nurtures stories grounded in care, collective responsibility, stewardship, and an abiding love for the natural world.Spanning countries, genres, languages, and forms, it brings together stories reimagining our relationship with all living things.

name: What Gets Funded Gets Published
description: Join us for a lecture in a bar, that asks a question most news consumers rarely pause to consider:Who really pays for the news you read?About the LectureFor most audiences today, journalism functions much like any other product or service, shaped by business models, incentives, and the financial structures that sustain it. Just like education, healthcare, or streaming platforms, news organisations must operate within economic systems that determine what survives and what disappears.So what happens when journalism is funded by corporate advertising? Or when governments become the primary source of financial support? But, there's a way for audiences to intersect - healthily, happily, with utility - with news organisations that aren't run by corporate or governments. This talk will explain how.About the SpeakerAbhinandan Sekhri is the cofounder and CEO of Newslaundry, one of India’s leading independent digital news platforms built on a subscription-driven model.He also cofounded Small Screen, a production house creating television, film, and digital content. Before launching Newslaundry, he worked as a producer, director, and writer on several television projects, including the award-winning food and travel show Highway on My Plate, as well as satirical news programmes such as Gustaakhi Maaf and The Great Indian Tamasha.Earlier in his career, he worked as a reporter with the India Today Group from 1995 to 1999.Through Newslaundry, he has been a vocal advocate for reader-funded journalism and media accountability in India.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Sanctuary in an Age of Suspicion & Surveillance
description: Join us for a lecture that asks a question both urgent and uncomfortable:Who decides what a refugee means?About the LectureDisplacement is not new. People have fled war, persecution, famine, and disaster for centuries. Yet today, the word refugee evokes anxiety, suspicion, and hostility across much of the world.In South Asia, a region shaped by partitions, migrations, and ongoing displacement, tensions between state sovereignty and refugee rights are intensifying. Anti-refugee sentiment feeds policy shifts, and policy shifts in turn reshape public perception. Meanwhile, the international refugee protection regime, once seen as a moral and legal achievement, is being steadily eroded, often by the very states that helped build it.What then is the future of refugee protection?This lecture traces the evolution of international refugee law, its conventions, norms, institutions, and political foundations, before turning to the contemporary landscape of displacement. It examines how states are responding to increasingly complex crises, and how narratives around refugees are being constructed, circulated, and weaponised.About the SpeakerDivita Shandilya is a Protection Associate at United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) in the Greater Delhi Area. Her work spans program management, protection research, and policy engagement in contexts of displacement and international development.Prior to her current role, she worked with ActionAid India, where she led policy and research initiatives focused on South-South cooperation and global governance. Her research interests include gender, refugee protection, international development architecture, and mechanisms of global cooperation. She has been closely involved in multi-country research on refugee women and their experiences of displacement and exclusion, and has contributed to international advocacy platforms including Civil20, BRICS Feminist Watch, and Civil BRICS.Her work sits at the intersection of policy, research, and lived experience — engaging both institutional frameworks and the human stories they seek to govern.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: Basement, Ground floor and First floor Building no. 12, Panchsheel Community Centre, Shahpur Jat, Panchsheel Park, New Delhi, Delhi 110017, India

name: Safdarjung Tomb Heritage Walk
description: Safdarjung’s Tomb Heritage WalkJoin us for a journey into the fading glow of Mughal splendour at Safdarjung's Tomb — often called the *last flicker of Mughal grandeur* in Delhi.Built in 1754 for Safdarjung, the powerful Nawab of Awadh and Prime Minister of the Mughal court, this monument stands at the twilight of an empire — grand in ambition, yet carrying within it the quiet signs of decline.Its story is as fascinating as its form. From courtly intrigues and shifting loyalties to the architectural choices that reveal a changing political landscape, Safdarjung’s life opens a compelling window into 18th-century Delhi.We will also trace a lesser-known layer of its history — how this tomb was constructed by reusing material from a pre-existing Mughal structure, quite literally building one memory over another.To truly understand Delhi — its rise, its resilience, and its gradual transitions — a visit to Safdarjung’s Tomb is essential.Come, walk with us through the final chapter of Mughal architectural brilliance — where grandeur meets gravity, and history lingers in every arch.

location.address: Airforce Golf Course, Delhi Race Club, New Delhi, Delhi 110003, India

name: Tracing Djnns of Kotla Ferozshah
description: TRACING THE DJINNS of FEROZSHAH KOTLA FORTAs twilight deepens and shadows lengthen across the old walls, we will gather at the main gate of Feroz Shah Kotla. Before us lie crumbling stone portals, hollowed arches, and silent courtyards that once bustled with life in the 14th century under Sultan Feroz Shah Tughlaq.This is no ordinary heritage walk. That evening, we will tread the boundary between history and myth — stepping into the realm of the djinns said to haunt these ruins, to listen, to judge, and sometimes to grant wishes.Led by expert storyteller and myth-hunter, the walk will weave together folklore, historical lore, and spooky tales when moonlight meets masonry. Expect atmospheric narration, dramatic pauses, unexpected twists — and maybe a few ghostly encounters.Not for the faint hearted !!Highlights * A guided evening walk through ruins, hidden chambers, and alleyways* Folklore and legends of djinns haunting the fort* Dramatic storytelling under the evening skies* Occasional “mystery stops” and whispers in the dark* A themed ritual (writing wishes / invoking legends) in the end.

description: DREAMCATCHERS THEATRE CLUB, GURGAONPRESENTSHE SAID SHE SAID A comedy of mannersFilled with laughter and drama with sharp wit as this classic comedy drama take you on a journey through the quirks of relationships, perceptions, and the misunderstandings that arise from everyday conversations. name: 4 sessions package TAI CHI WITH RASHID
description: VALID FOR 4 SESSIONS UNTIL END OF MAY 2026 Join the Tai Chi Practice Circle with sensei Rashid Ansari for cultivating wellness, calmness, grounding and inner balance with the transformative practices of Tai Chi and Qi Gong. Held on Sundays at Lilanoor Centre. Hosted by IT'S ALL GOOD.Starting March 15th.Cost : Rs. 4800/- for 4 sessions (Student Rs. 2000/-). IMPORTANT NOTE: To book this 4-SESSION PAGKAGE, please click on the session given: MAY 31st. It means that you may attend ANY 4 Sunday Tai Chi sessions until the end of May, 2026. Thank you. [You may also book a single session as a DROP-IN (Rs. 1500/- per session; Student Rs. 600/-). See specific date in the main listing for booking, or simply drop in and pay on the spot.] ***Both Tai Chi and Qi Gong offer a unique perspective of being a Participant and Observer simultaneously.Tai chi, often called Meditation in Motion, is an incredibly potent system of mind body synergy and a block and tackle system, working on the joints and angles of the body.regulates blood pressure and hypertension.the different movements work on the different organs and glands, helping and energising them to work at optimum potency.strengthens the muscles and tendons, aligns the skeletal and neuromuscular system.helps to deal with anxiety and mental health issues.can be practiced by anybody - young and old alike.Rashid Ansari has been practicing/teaching the martial arts , dance, movement, choreography and holistic health/wellness for over 50 years now, both in India and various parts of the world. In addition to martial arts, Ansari is a contemporary dance instructor and choreographer. He has explored physical theatre and theatre movement, integrating these disciplines into his teaching methodology. He founded and serves as the artistic director of Mobius Strip, a dance theatre company, and Earth Rhythms, a world music ensemble. He also conducts classes and workshops in “Dance your Senses - The 5 Rhythms.” A organic movement methodology based on the individual’s own intrinsic sense of movement and emotions - as a means of finding one’s own movement and dealing with physical, emotional and intellectual blocks.***IT'S ALL GOOD | The Science and Art of JoyMany people feel stuck, exasperated, low, or just plain bored in their lives. We provide a platform for fun and engaging experiences that foster insight and empowerment.Connect | Learn | Thrive!INSTAGRAM LINK CLICK HERE

name: Curated Tour of Lodhi Art District by St+art India
description: Lodhi Art DistrictLodhi Art District is India’s first ongoing art district. An open-air museum by St+art India Foundation, the pedestrian-friendly colony houses over 65+ murals by renowned local and international artists. The area serves as evidence of the possibility of reimagining public spaces, bringing us back in touch with our city, its history, and the many narratives that illuminate it. Sign up for an immersive walk guided by a St+art team member to discover the murals, consider how public spaces can be utilised, and become a part of this cultural transformation.Meeting Point: Indian Habitat Centre, Gate 3About the TourThe curated tours are a walkthrough of the vibrant public art district. They offer an interactive exploration through which St+art introduces new ways of experiencing contemporary art and the cityscape. These tours provide a complete experience, delving into the historical aspects of the location, the stories behind the project, and the murals themselves. Participants will actively witness the enhancement of the city and the reimagining of its public spaces. A tour leader from the St+art team will provide insights into the background and context of the artworks, the area, and its cultural significance in the city. (Nearby attractions include Safdarjung Tomb, Lodhi Garden, and Khan Market). About St+artSt+art India Foundation contributes to urban regeneration and community living through contemporary urban art projects. The foundation enables a vision for democratised public spaces through interdisciplinary art interventions that are rooted in the social context.Since 2014, with the support of Asian paints, the foundation has organised multiple festivals and public art projects in Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Chandigarh and Coimbatore among others. Each public art district in the country and editions of festivals bring curated interventions to civic spaces that are embedded in urban culture, and use art as a tool to reimagine how public spaces can be utilised. For the foundation, which has been responsible for making urban art a movement in India, public art interventions are a celebration of the street as a canvas for visual creativity.

location.address: India Habitat Centre Gate 3, Vardhman Marg, Block 20, Institutional Area, Lodi Colony, New Delhi, Delhi 110003, India

name: TAI CHI WITH RASHID Drop In Classes
description: Join the Tai Chi Practice Circle with sensei Rashid Ansari for cultivating wellness, calmness, grounding and inner balance with the transformative practices of Tai Chi and Qi Gong. Held every Sunday at Lilanoor Centre. Hosted by IT'S ALL GOOD.Cost : Rs. 1500/- per session (Student Rs. 600/-)Monthly packages also available. Please see main listing.***Both Tai Chi and Qi Gong offer a unique perspective of being a Participant and Observer simultaneously.Tai chi, often called Meditation in Motion, is an incredibly potent system of mind body synergy and a block and tackle system, working on the joints and angles of the body.regulates blood pressure and hypertension.the different movements work on the different organs and glands, helping and energising them to work at optimum potency.strengthens the muscles and tendons, aligns the skeletal and neuromuscular system.helps to deal with anxiety and mental health issues.can be practiced by anybody - young and old alike.Rashid Ansari has been practicing/teaching the martial arts , dance, movement, choreography and holistic health/wellness for over 50 years now, both in India and various parts of the world. In addition to martial arts, Ansari is a contemporary dance instructor and choreographer. He has explored physical theatre and theatre movement, integrating these disciplines into his teaching methodology. He founded and serves as the artistic director of Mobius Strip, a dance theatre company, and Earth Rhythms, a world music ensemble. He also conducts classes and workshops in “Dance your Senses - The 5 Rhythms.” A organic movement methodology based on the individual’s own intrinsic sense of movement and emotions - as a means of finding one’s own movement and dealing with physical, emotional and intellectual blocks.***IT'S ALL GOOD | The Science and Art of JoyMany people feel stuck, exasperated, low, or just plain bored in their lives. We provide a platform for fun and engaging experiences that foster insight and empowerment.Connect | Learn | Thrive!INSTAGRAM
